name: PyGWalker (Tableau-like in Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Kanaries; cloud hosting available as paid service

auth.notes: Local library for Jupyter; cloud features require Kanaries account

performance.notes: Client-side rendering in browser; DuckDB-Wasm for large datasets in newer versions

agent_readiness.notes: Turns pandas DataFrames into a Tableau-like drag-and-drop visualization interface inside Jupyter notebooks. pyg.walk(df) renders an interactive BI tool. Drag fields to axes, choose chart types, filter, group. Supports pandas, Polars, Modin, Spark. Graphic Walker (Vega-based) as rendering engine. DuckDB-Wasm backend for large datasets. Export visualizations as code or images. Integrated with Streamlit via streamlit-pygwalker.

gotchas: Requires Jupyter for full interactive experience
Larger JavaScript bundle than simpler alternatives
best_when: Tableau-like drag-and-drop visualization in Jupyter notebooks
Business users wanting BI tool inside Python notebooks
Quick exploration without writing chart code
Streamlit dashboards needing interactive BI components
avoid_when: Production dashboard serving (use full BI tool or Panel/Streamlit)
summary: PyGWalker renders a Tableau-like drag-and-drop BI interface inside Jupyter notebooks. pyg.walk(df) opens an interactive chart builder with Vega rendering. Supports pandas, Polars, Spark. DuckDB-Wasm backend for larger datasets. Integrates with Streamlit. Best for business users wanting BI tool ergonomics inside Python notebooks without Tableau or Power BI licenses.
